data.title: Senator Tillis' Opposition Challenges Trump's Fed Chair Plans
data.description: Republican Senator Thom Tillis has emerged as a significant obstacle for U.S. President Donald Trump in his efforts to appoint a Federal Reserve chair who aligns with his preference for lower interest rates. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Tillis' resistance, which complicates Trump's strategy to influence monetary policy. This development underscores the ongoing tension between the executive branch and the Federal Reserve over interest rate policies.

data.title: Bitcoin's Recent Decline Among Top Five Largest Crashes in History
data.description: Bitcoin has experienced one of its most significant declines, ranking among the top five largest crashes in its history. According to NS3.AI, this recent downturn is comparable to the major crash of 2021. Such large-scale declines are uncommon for Bitcoin, underscoring the severity of the current losses. The on-chain data platform highlighted that this drop is positioned between the third and fifth largest ever recorded.

data.title: Gold and Silver Prices Plummet Amid U.S. Financial Stress
data.description:
Gold and silver prices have experienced a significant decline as financial stress in the United States intensifies, marked by record household debt and a rise in corporate bankruptcies. According to NS3.AI, the sell-off in these metals is occurring alongside economic indicators reminiscent of those seen before previous recessions. Bitcoin has also seen a downturn, failing to serve as a safe-haven asset during this period of economic uncertainty.

Analysts are currently divided on the implications of this price correction. Some suggest it could indicate a multi-year repricing, while others believe it may be a temporary setback. The markets are closely monitoring potential policy easing by the Federal Reserve, anticipated later in 2026, which could influence future price movements.

data.title: European Space Agency Launches Powerful Ariane 6 Rocket for Amazon Satellites
data.description: The European Space Agency successfully launched the most advanced version of its Ariane 6 rocket on Thursday, aiming to deploy numerous Amazon Leo satellites into orbit. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Jeff Bezos's strategic move to enhance his competition against Elon Musk's Starlink. This launch marks a significant step in Amazon's efforts to expand its satellite network, which is designed to provide global internet coverage. The Ariane 6 rocket, known for its increased payload capacity and efficiency, is expected to play a crucial role in future satellite deployments. As the space race intensifies, both Amazon and SpaceX continue to invest heavily in their satellite technologies to secure a competitive edge in the burgeoning market for satellite internet services.

data.title: Senate advances major boost for faster satellite approvals
data.description:
A key Senate committee voted Thursday to approve a plan that would break through the red tape that has slowed the development of satellite-based broadband across the United States, potentially reshaping internet access for millions of Americans in rural places. The Senate Commerce Committee passed the measure following amendments pushed by Senator Maria Cantwell, the […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/senate-advances-satellite-approval/

data.title: ETH ETF holders in ‘worse position’ than BTC ETF peers as crypto market looks for bottom
data.description:
Bitcoin and Ether spot ETF holders are nursing steep losses as the market continues to search for a local bottom, but data from Bloomberg suggests neither cohort is capitulating.
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/eth-etf-holders-in-worse-position-than-btc-etf-peers-as-crypto-market-looks-for-bottom?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

data.title: Software Stocks and Crypto Face Challenges
data.description: Jim Bianco, president of Bianco Research posted on X, noted that software stocks are struggling again. $IGV (iShares Software ETF) is near last week's lows. He compared software to 'programmable money,' like crypto, suggesting they are similar.

data.title: Russia to Launch Digital Ruble for BRICS Trade by September 1
data.description: Russia is set to introduce its digital ruble for trade transactions with BRICS countries, aiming for a launch date of September 1. According to NS3.AI, despite limited domestic interest from individuals, businesses, and banks, the digital ruble is primarily viewed as an international project to enhance cross-border trade. This move is part of a broader trend among BRICS nations to adopt central bank digital currencies (CBDCs) to improve trade efficiency.

data.title: US IPO Market Faces Setbacks Amid Technology Losses
data.description: The U.S. IPO market experienced turbulence last week as newly public companies resulted in losses for investors who had invested in technology startups. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting concerns that these setbacks could dampen the optimistic atmosphere surrounding major listings anticipated later this year. The recent performance of these IPOs has raised questions about the sustainability of the current market enthusiasm, especially as investors remain cautious about the potential risks associated with technology ventures. Market analysts are closely monitoring the situation, as the outcome of these listings could influence investor sentiment and the broader market outlook in the coming months.

data.title: EU to Revise Merger Rules to Facilitate Pan-European Deals
data.description: The European Union is undertaking a comprehensive revision of its merger regulations to promote transactions across Europe. According to Jin10, the EU's antitrust regulators will now consider factors such as innovation, sustainability, resilience, investment, and employment when evaluating mergers. This move aims to streamline processes and encourage more cross-border deals within the EU.

data.title: Ethereum Staking Hits Record 36.8M ETH Locked – Here Is Why Price Isn’t Rallying Yet
data.description:
Ethereum staking has reached an impressive milestone, with a record 36.8 million ETH now locked in the staking process. However, despite this significant development, the price of Ethereum has not experienced a rally as many investors might have anticipated. In this article, we will explore the factors contributing to the current price stagnation, including market sentiment, external economic conditions, and potential regulatory considerations that may be impacting investor behavior in the cryptocurrency space.
source: https://blocknews.com/ethereum-staking-hits-record-36-8m-eth-locked-here-is-why-price-isnt-rallying-yet/

data.title: Messier Advances in Privacy and Wallet Integration Amid Market Distractions
data.description: Crypto Rover posted on X that while the market is preoccupied with various distractions, Messier is making significant progress in developing its architecture. The company is prioritizing compliance-first privacy solutions and is working on integrating unified wallets and native capabilities. This development comes as the market continues to focus on other noise, highlighting Messier's commitment to innovation in the crypto space.

data.title: Concerns Raised Over Gambling Site Allowing Bets on Military Attacks
data.description: A gambling platform that permits soldiers to wager on military attacks is raising significant concerns. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the potential negative outcomes of such activities. The site, which allows bets on the occurrence and outcomes of military operations, has sparked debate over its ethical implications and the possible impact on military conduct. Critics argue that the platform could incentivize reckless behavior and undermine the seriousness of military engagements. The controversy surrounding the site underscores the need for careful consideration of the intersection between gambling and military activities.

data.title: S&P 500's Performance on Friday the 13th in February Shows Historical Weakness
data.description: Jeffrey Hirsch, CEO of Hirsch Holdings and publisher of the Almanac Trader, posted on X that the S&P 500 has experienced declines on eight out of thirteen Friday the 13th days in February since 1930, with an average loss of 0.05%. In February 2015, the index saw a slight increase of 0.41%, despite having fallen on six of the previous seven occurrences. This pattern is likely attributed to the timing of Friday the 13th, which often coincides with the historically weak period around Presidents’ Day, rather than any superstition.

data.title: Blackstone Appoints Thomas Schleicher as Senior Adviser for Nordic Region
data.description: Blackstone has appointed Thomas Schleicher as a senior adviser for the Nordic region. Bloomberg posted on X that Schleicher previously managed funds for the billionaire owners of Lego. This strategic move aims to strengthen Blackstone's presence and operations in the Nordic market. Schleicher's extensive experience in financial management is expected to contribute significantly to Blackstone's growth and investment strategies in the region.

data.title: Dutch House of Representatives Approves 36% Unrealized Gains Tax
data.description: Crypto Rover posted on X that the Dutch House of Representatives has passed a new tax measure imposing a 36% tax on unrealized gains. This development marks a significant change in the country's tax policy, potentially impacting investors and financial markets. The decision has sparked discussions among stakeholders about its implications for the Dutch economy and investment landscape.

data.title: The Real Battle for Bitcoin and Stablecoins Begins
data.description: Scott Melker, host of The Wolf Of All Streets Podcast, posted on X about the ongoing battle for Bitcoin and stablecoins. He highlighted insights from Paolo Ardoino and Bo Hines, urging viewers to watch the discussion.

data.title: EU Commission President Calls for Completion of Savings and Investment Union by June
data.description: The European Union (EU) is urged to complete the first phase of its Savings and Investment Union by June, according to EU Commission President Ursula von der Leyen. According to Jin10, von der Leyen emphasized the importance of progress in this initiative, which aims to enhance financial integration and investment across member states. She warned that if no significant advancements are made by June, at least nine EU member countries might pursue enhanced cooperation independently. This move is seen as a critical step in strengthening the EU's economic framework and fostering greater financial stability within the region.

data.title: Cardano Partners with LayerZero for Blockchain Interoperability
data.description: Cardano has announced a partnership with LayerZero to enhance interoperability with over 160 blockchains, granting access to more than $80 billion in omnichain assets. According to NS3.AI, this integration allows Cardano to overcome its architectural differences and connect with major ecosystems without altering its foundational model. Despite the technical advancements, Cardano's potential to attract substantial liquidity and achieve sustained growth in decentralized finance (DeFi) remains uncertain and depends on future adoption.
